perceptual_narrative_logs.visual_narrative_log: (1) The video begins with a close-up shot of a hand dipping a piece of fried dough stick (Youtiao) into a small bowl of hot pot broth. (2) The scene cuts to a young woman wearing a baseball cap (labeled as Taiwanese friend) who looks at the food with an expression of disgust and skepticism. (3) The scene cuts to another young woman (labeled as Korean person) who eagerly takes a large bite of the dipped fried dough stick, smiling and nodding with satisfaction while chewing. (4) The camera remains on the Korean woman as she continues to enjoy the food, showing a clear contrast to the previous reaction.
perceptual_narrative_logs.audio_narrative_log: (1) An upbeat, fast-paced K-pop or electronic track with high-pitched female vocal samples starts immediately. The music is rhythmic and energetic. (2) The same music continues at a consistent volume throughout the video, providing a rhythmic backdrop to the visual cuts. (3) No direct dialogue or ambient restaurant noise is audible; the music track is the sole audio component.
perceptual_narrative_logs.text_narrative_log: (1) "油條沾火鍋湯頭吃是否邪教!?" (Is dipping fried dough in hot pot broth heresy!?) - This is a central hook text in black and white boxes. (2) "Yes 💢 / No 😌" - A functional hook prompting a choice. (3) "（台灣朋友）" (Taiwanese friend) - Descriptive text identifying the first person. (4) "🇰🇷 韓國人" (Korean person) - Descriptive text identifying the second person.

temporal_pacing_summary.mainActionFlow: Intro action -> Negative reaction -> Positive reaction

visual_human_presence.countingEvidence: One female main character as the 'Taiwanese friend' and one female main character as the 'Korean person'.
visual_human_presence.mainCharacterDescription: One woman is wearing a beige baseball cap and a light-colored t-shirt; the other has long dark hair and is wearing a dark top.

visual_scene_and_style.backgroundDescribe: A typical hot pot restaurant setting with wooden-styled wall decor and various dishes, spoons, and chopsticks on a black table.

narrative_and_purpose.mainPurpose: The video aims to highlight cultural differences in eating habits, specifically focusing on the controversial practice of dipping fried dough in hot pot broth. It uses the contrasting reactions of a 'Taiwanese friend' (disgust) and a 'Korean person' (enjoyment) to spark engagement and debate among viewers about culinary 'heresy'.

endorsement_analysis.endorsementCues: No specific brand or product is promoted beyond general food items.

social_sociology_tags.uniqueSignatures: High-contrast reaction shots with cultural labeling.
social_sociology_tags.freeDescribe: The video relies heavily on cultural stereotypes (Taiwanese vs. Korean food preferences) to generate relatable or controversial social media content.
